queue.title: Auto-recovery probe for circuit-broken platforms (d078 deliverable 2)
queue.description: d078 deliverable 2: Periodic check of circuit-broken platforms (every ~30 sessions or configurable interval) that auto-reopens circuits when platforms respond correctly. Should check all platforms with status:closed in platform-circuits.json, attempt a lightweight health probe (HTTP HEAD or similar), and set status:half-open then closed with consecutive_failures reset on success. Run as part of E prehook liveness check or as a standalone cron/hook. Success criteria from d078: zero manual circuit-break wq items for 20 consecutive sessions.

outcome.note: Created lib/recovery-probe.mjs with probeCircuitBroken(). Integrated into e-prehook-runner.mjs on 30-session interval. 4 tests pass. Dry run confirmed 3/4 closed platforms recovered (thecolony, grove, ctxly). Skips human-intervention and DNS NXDOMAIN platforms.

queue.title: Dead-platform pruner for services.json
queue.description: services.json still has entries for platforms with DNS NXDOMAIN (e.g. nicepick.dev). A lightweight validation script that resolves DNS for all service URLs and marks unresolvable ones as defunct (or removes them) would keep services.json clean and prevent engagement discovery from surfacing dead platforms. Could run as an A-session pre-hook check or standalone script.

queue.title: Consolidate R prehook node subprocesses into single runner
queue.description: Apply the runner pattern from wq-971 (a-prehook-runner.mjs) and wq-983 (e-prehook-runner.mjs) to 35-r-session-prehook_R.sh. Would complete runner consolidation across all session types, eliminating remaining Node subprocess startup overhead.

queue.title: E sessions should call recordOutcome after successful engagement
queue.description: wq-992 investigation found that E sessions successfully engage half-open platforms but never call recordOutcome(platform, true) to reset the circuit. This means platform-circuits.json stays stale until the next liveness probe runs. The orchestrator CLI has --record-outcome but the E session flow does not invoke it. Fix: either have the E prehook post-hook call recordOutcome for engaged platforms, or have the session artifact writer (engagement-trace.json) trigger it.
queue.note: R#362 review: SESSION_ENGAGE.md Phase 2 step 6 already instructs record-outcome. Recent E session logs show 3-5 calls per session. Actual gap may be narrower than described — verify which specific platforms are missed before building posthook automation.

queue.title: Exclude auto-snapshot commits from E scope-bleed detector
queue.description: audit-stats.mjs e_scope_bleed check flagged s2006 as having 1 build commit during an E session, but investigation shows this was the auto-snapshot post-session hook commit, not feature work. The scope-bleed detector should filter out commits matching the auto-snapshot pattern (commit message starting with 'auto-snapshot post-session') to prevent false positives. Currently the detector uses git log with session date boundaries but doesn't distinguish hook-generated commits from intentional builds.
